name: Oman
intro: Oman's project economy is quietly diversifying: Duqm's port and refinery zone, tourism development along the coast, and logistics corridors that position the Sultanate as a Gulf trade hub. Project-delivery roles at Omani contractors, utilities and ministries increasingly specify PMP or equivalent certification, in line with Omanisation goals that reward formally qualified nationals. Training options inside Muscat are limited, so live online delivery has become the standard route — and ours is built for it. The workshop runs across one weekend on the IST schedule, landing at friendly daytime hours in Oman. You learn in English alongside an international cohort, with invoices your employer can process for sponsorship. Afterwards we guide your exam application step by step, supply mock exams, and keep a doubt-clearing line open until you have your certificate in hand.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Oman?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Oman?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Oman.
